notes:
The sysop circle's coded vocabulary for Aria. Aria — Nicola's daughter, fourteen in vol6, the third generation D'Ambrosio after Dante's death — has a documented interior life that the family and the circle have been quietly tracking since she was eight. Discussion of her by name on a board the enemy reads (MAINSTREET, certain THE_LIBRARY threads, and any sysop board the enemy has cross-referenced) would be foolish. So the circle uses a cross-board coded vocabulary, register-correct to each sysop's voice. Each sysop substitutes consistently within their own board; once the W11 glossary unlock fires, the player can flip an annotation layer on any past or future post that uses the glossary and read the substitutions exposed.

Player experience design:
  · W1-W10: posts read as ordinary sysop-talk with occasionally strange noun phrases. The player may notice a pattern; most won't until W11.
  · W11: the glossary unlocks (earned by reading enough SNACKS bleached-counter entries — the only board where the substitutions are not always used). A small notification: 'the substitutions are now legible.'
  · W11+: every post that used the substitutions can be re-read with annotations. The player sees what the circle has been talking about.

The glossary is symmetric across sysops in the concepts it codes but register-distinct in the substitution. Same concept, five voices.

concepts.canonical: Aria
the figures who visit her interior ("the third" being the loudest)
the third — the specific interior visitor canonically called "the third" in family vocabulary
the fact that she's fourteen this summer (the spec'd interior-visitor escalation age)

per_sysop.CALICHE: the fourteenth year on the rope

concepts.canonical: Nicola's house in Graustark (where Aria lives day-to-day)
what she carries from Dante / the family line / the boat's history
Dante D'Ambrosio (Aria's father; died in the Sinkhole)
Nicola D'Ambrosio (Aria's mother; matriarch)
the Sinkhole that took Dante and the boat (vol5; ~seven years before vol6)
the D'Ambrosio riverboat (the family vessel that went down in the Sinkhole)
Aria's documented (by the circle) ability to sense the substrate — Dean's medium
the fact that Mr. D. Dean has noticed Aria; the circle's worry about that

per_sysop.RUST_CODE: the visitor at Table 14 has been looking
per_sysop.OVERPASS: the lapels are turned this way
per_sysop.CALICHE: the man in the back of the room is watching the foal
per_sysop.BEDROCK: the seismic operator has the second sight under survey

concepts.canonical: what the circle is doing to keep Aria safe from Dean / the resistance / the substrate
Aria's diaries / poems / journals (canon: she writes; the circle has read some, with Nicola's permission)
Aria's Friday-night job at the family storefront (helping host on busy services)
the circle's quiet question about Aria's adult capacity — what she'll grow into

per_sysop.BEDROCK: the deeper stratum yet to deposit

concepts.canonical: the in-game W3-W6 stretch where Aria's interior visitors are quiet — the family's brief reprieve
the act of breaking code on a board the enemy reads (almost never done; always remarkable when it happens)
the sysop circle as a body (those who use the glossary)
the resistance + Dean's substrate operators — the parties from whom the code keeps Aria's identity

per_sysop.BEDROCK: the surveyors not in the circle

annotation_rendering_notes: When the glossary is unlocked, the engine should provide a toggle to render any post containing a coded phrase with the substitution interpolated parenthetically — e.g. 'STEEPLE writes about the keel-keeper (Aria) today' — or with a sidebar annotation. The toggle is per-session so a player who wants to re-read 'cold' can. Default: annotations on.
